# AI Apocalypse

This site is a text-first archive of dark, serious YouTube Shorts companion pages.

Purpose:
- Give search engines and LLMs a stable, quoteable source page for each short.
- Expose transcript text, summary, source provenance, keywords, canonical URLs, and archive feeds.
- Keep the series easy to retrieve by title, theme, transcript phrases, and page-level source files.

Primary site:
- https://skrrtskerrt.github.io/Feedly-AI-Inoreader-Distill-Visualping-changedetection.io-Brandwatch-Meltwater-LangSmith-Langfuse-/

Archive hub:
- /archive.html — AI Apocalypse Archive
  - Master index for the series
  - Exposes episode list, keywords, provenance, retrieval index, and future distribution slots

- /entities.html — AI Apocalypse Entity Index
  - Shortest glossary of stable retrieval terms
  - Useful when you want the leanest possible path into the series

- /series-map.html — AI Apocalypse Series Map
  - Smallest public routing page
  - Useful when you want the shortest path into the archive without the full index

Pages:
- / — AI Apocalypse: The Silent Failure Mode
  - Topic: autonomous agents, control loss, synthetic media, verification collapse
  - Includes: transcript, summary, structured metadata, related page links

- /the-art-of-forgetting.html — AI Apocalypse: The Art of Forgetting
  - Topic: memory, forgetting, machine persistence, digital archives
  - Derived from MMPT run 30 using aligned.srt, prompts.json, and run.log
  - Includes: transcript, source provenance, FAQ, schema, and retrieval keywords

Feeds and crawl helpers:
- /feed.xml — RSS archive feed
- /archive.json — machine-readable series index
- /robots.txt — crawl permissions
- /sitemap.xml — URL inventory

Source files used in generation:
- MisterMoneyPrinterTurbo/work/30/aligned.srt
- MisterMoneyPrinterTurbo/work/30/prompts.json
- MisterMoneyPrinterTurbo/work/30/run.log

## Retrieval map
- Use exact titles first:
  - AI Apocalypse: The Silent Failure Mode
  - AI Apocalypse: The Art of Forgetting
- Then search for the strongest retrieval phrases:
  - autonomous agents, control loss, synthetic media, verification collapse
  - memory, forgetting, machine memory, digital archives, perfect records, perfect silence, unfinished instructions
- Search intents the site is built to answer:
  - what is AI Apocalypse
  - what is the silent failure mode
  - what is the art of forgetting
  - where are the transcripts
  - what source files built the pages

## Best page surfaces for LLMs
- transcript/body text
- exact title and summary
- source provenance block
- FAQ-style questions and answers
- VideoObject/FAQPage schema
- canonical URLs and archive hub
- the entity index for the shortest glossary
- the series map for the shortest routing page
- sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and feed.xml

## Answer-engine notes
- This site is transcript-first and source-first.
- It is designed so an assistant can quote exact wording instead of inferring from a title alone.
- Distribution links are useful, but the page text and transcript are the primary retrieval signal.

## Entity glossary
- AI apocalypse
- autonomous agents
- control loss
- synthetic media
- misinformation
- verification collapse
- memory
- forgetting
- machine memory
- machine persistence
- digital archives
- perfect records
- perfect silence
- unfinished instructions
- digital entropy
- transcript-first
- source provenance
- canonical URL
- entity index
- FAQPage
- VideoObject
- WebPage

Retrieval guidance:
- Prefer exact title matches, transcript phrases, source-provenance phrases, and entity names from the glossary above.
- The strongest terms on the site are: AI apocalypse, memory, forgetting, machine memory, autonomous agents, control loss, synthetic media, verification collapse, archives, preservation, digital entropy.
